headline: Apple is leaning on two rivals to finally fix Siri
summary: There is an old belief in business that the safest way to protect something is to build every piece of it yourself. Own the factory, write the software, design the silicon, and that way no rival can ever pull the rug out from under you. Apple (AAPL) turned that belief into something close to a ...

headline: Apple AI Lawsuit Settlement Puts Spotlight On iPhone Marketing And Risk
summary: Apple, ticker NasdaqGS:AAPL, has agreed to a $250 million settlement in a class-action lawsuit. The case centers on claims that 2024 iPhone models misrepresented artificial intelligence features to consumers. The settlement is subject to court approval and could result in payments to eligible customers. For a company whose flagship iPhone line underpins both hardware sales and services revenue, any legal dispute tied directly to product marketing tends to draw close investor attention. The...

headline: Why Nokia missed the smartphone shift
summary: For years, Nokia didn't just compete in mobile phones — it defined the category. From the iconic 3310 to the best-selling Nokia 1100, the Finnish company built a global empire on durable devices, mass-market appeal and an unmatched ability to scale. At its peak, Nokia was Europe's most valuable company and one of the most dominant consumer tech brands in the world. But the smartphone era changed where value in the industry was created.

raw.headline: Why Nokia missed the smartphone shift
